idea: Build ShiftPulse, a lightweight shift-handoff board for a fictional regional manufacturing company. Supervisors capture unresolved operational issues before a shift ends, and the incoming shift can review ownership, urgency, status, and the next required action from one screen.
audience: Manufacturing shift supervisors and facilities operations coordinators
outcome: Reduce missed handoff items and make it immediately clear which operational issues need attention, who owns the next action, and what has already been completed.
constraints: Run entirely locally with no backend, authentication, network calls, or external services
Use fictional seeded data only and never include sensitive operational information
Keep the primary demo workflow understandable in under three minutes
must_have_capabilities: View all shift handoff items with owner, urgency, status, and next action
Filter handoff items by status and urgency
Advance an item from open to in progress to resolved
See summary counts for total, attention needed, and resolved items
visual_direction: A polished enterprise operations console with strong hierarchy, high contrast, clear status colors, and a calm industrial visual character.
demo_preferences: Show the queue, filter urgent work, advance one handoff item, and confirm the summary counts update.
